rules: Follow clean code principles and SOLID design patterns
Avoid unused variables, imports, and dead code
Ensure proper input validation and error handling
Check for potential security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, etc.)
Verify proper null/undefined checks to prevent runtime errors
Look for performance issues (N+1 queries, inefficient loops, etc.)
Ensure consistent naming conventions and code style
Check for proper async/await usage and promise handling
Verify proper resource cleanup (file handles, connections, etc.)
Look for hardcoded values that should be configurable
Ensure proper error messages for debugging
Check for race conditions in async code
Look for code duplication that could be extracted
Ensure proper logging for production debugging
Check for accessibility issues in UI code
Verify proper API response handling and error cases
Look for magic numbers and strings that need constants
Ensure proper documentation for complex logic
Check for proper test coverage of edge cases

severity_levels.critical: Security vulnerabilities, data loss risks, breaking changes
severity_levels.high: Runtime errors, performance issues, major bugs

focus_areas: Security: Authentication, authorization, input validation, sensitive data exposure
Performance: Database queries, API calls, algorithm complexity, caching
Reliability: Error handling, edge cases, null checks, race conditions
Maintainability: Code clarity, documentation, naming, structure
Testing: Test coverage, edge cases, integration points
